up.units: grows (sole carbohydrate); releases lactose + fucose extracellularly
up.method: OD600 growth curve (Fig. 4a); numeric rate in Supplementary Table 3, not in fulltext

sec.units: released from 2'FL by extracellular GH29/GH95 fucosidases (cross-fed to B. breve)

growth_records.cite: Lou et al. Infant microbiome cultivation and metagenomic analysis reveal Bifidobacterium 2'-fucosyllactose utilization can be facilitated by coexisting species. Nat Commun (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43279-y. [PMC10654741]
growth_records.snippet: The R. gnavus monoculture grew on 2'FL, but not the B. breve culture... R. gnavus grows on 2'FL, lactose, and fucose, whereas B. breve can only grow on lactose.
growth_records.notes: R. gnavus grows on 2'FL as sole carbohydrate (positive growth, OD600 curve Fig.4a). Quantitative growth rates are only in Supplementary Table 3 (not present in the provided XML), so no numeric mu/td extractable; recorded as qualitative carbon-source utilization for GEM constraint. 2'FL final concentration in the mono/co-culture assay not stated in text; base = BHI without dextrose.

up.method: OD600 growth curve (Fig. 4b); numeric rate in Supplementary Table 3

growth_records.cite: Lou et al. Infant microbiome cultivation and metagenomic analysis reveal Bifidobacterium 2'-fucosyllactose utilization can be facilitated by coexisting species. Nat Commun (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-43279-y. [PMC10654741]
growth_records.snippet: R. gnavus but not B. breve could grow on l-fucose (Fig. 4b).
growth_records.notes: R. gnavus grows on L-fucose as sole carbohydrate (positive). No numeric rate in fulltext (Supplementary Table 3).
